key_insight: Neural operators can learn the inverse mapping from full-field displacement and force data directly to physically admissible constitutive laws, enabling rapid material model discovery.
engineering_relevance: This could replace slow, iterative calibration with near-instantaneous constitutive identification from experimental measurements in solid mechanics.
method: PANO adds physics constraints while mapping measured displacements and reaction forces to strain-energy functions.
CANO learns constitutive functions in a constrained output space to ensure physically admissible material behavior.
Laplacian eigenfunction encoding makes the approach more robust to discretization changes and noise.
evidence_signal: The models were trained on simulated displacement-force tuples across multiple material models and tested on unseen, noisy, partially missing, and differently discretized data as well as different geometries.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training simulations cover the range of real experimental conditions and material behaviors.
paper_title: Neural operators solve inverse problems for constitutive model discovery
